userStories.title: nax config --explain
userStories.description: Add `nax config` CLI command with `--explain` flag that dumps the effective merged config with inline comments. Resolution order: defaults → global (~/.nax/config.json) → project (nax/config.json) → CLI overrides. Each field gets a one-line human-readable description of what it does. Fields overridden by project config are annotated with '(project override)' vs '(global)' vs '(default)'. Output format: YAML-like with # comments. Must cover all sections: models, routing, execution, tdd, quality, review, context, constitution, analyze, plan, acceptance, interaction, autoMode. Implementation: add `src/cli/config.ts` with `configCommand()`. Read merged config from loader, annotate each field using a static descriptions map (Record<string, string>). Register in `src/cli/index.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `nax config --explain` outputs all config fields with descriptions
Each field shows its source: (default), (global), or (project override)
Output is human-readable, not raw JSON
Works from any directory (uses existing config loader)

userStories.title: nax config --diff
userStories.description: Add `--diff` flag to `nax config` that shows only fields where project config overrides the global config. Output format: table or structured list showing field path, project value, and global value. Skip fields that are identical or only set at one layer. If no project config exists, print 'No project config found — using global defaults'. Implementation: in `src/cli/config.ts`, load global and project configs separately (extend loader to expose both), deep-diff them, format as table. Use the same descriptions map from CM-001 to add context.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `nax config --diff` shows only fields where project differs from global
Each differing field shows: field path, project value, global value
Shows 'No project config found' when no nax/config.json exists

userStories.title: nax config (default view)
userStories.description: When `nax config` is run without flags, print the effective merged config as formatted JSON with a header showing the config sources found (global path, project path if exists). This is the quick 'what am I actually running with?' view. Implementation: default action in `src/cli/config.ts`. Load merged config, pretty-print with JSON.stringify(config, null, 2). Add header lines showing which config files were found and loaded.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `nax config` prints the effective merged config as formatted JSON
Header shows paths of config files found (global, project)
Missing config files noted in header
